summary: Fluoride overconsumption can interfere with proper enamel crystalline structures causing damage or weakness; whereas, cleidocranial dysostosis mainly impacts skeletal growth without affecting early dental tissue sufficiently for inducing specific enamel hypoplasias pertinent here.
